zh.lifeSummary: Johannah Child於1434年出生在英國武斯特斯爾伍斯特。她於大約1464年與William Childe在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1511年在英國武斯特斯爾諾維其可享年77歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Johannah Child于1434年出生在英国武斯特斯爾伍斯特。她于大约1464年与William Childe在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1511年在英国武斯特斯爾諾維其可享年77岁。

nameMeanings.name: Child
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: nickname from Middle English child ‘child, infant’ (Old English cild), in various possible applications. The word is found in Old English as a byname, and in Middle English as a widely used affectionate term of address. It was also used as a term of status for a young man of noble birth, although the exact meaning is not clear; in the 13th and 14th centuries it was a technical term used of a young noble awaiting elevation to the knighthood. In other cases it may have been applied as a byname to a youth considerably younger than his brothers or to one who was a minor on the death of his father.
in Kent, possibly a topographic name from Old English cielde ‘spring (water)’, a rare word derived from c(e)ald ‘cold’.

variants.name: Barnes
meanings: English: habitational name from Barnes (on the Surrey bank of the Thames in London), named with Old English bere-ærn ‘barn, a storehouse for barley and other grain’, or a topographic name or metonymic occupational name for someone who lived by or worked at a barn or barns, from Middle English barn ‘barn, granary’.

English: variant of Barne, with excrescent -s, derived from either the Middle English personal name Bern, Barn (based on the Scandinavian personal name Biǫrn or Old English Beorn, both from a word meaning ‘warrior’), or from Middle English barn (Old Norse barn) ‘child’. The latter term is found as a byname for men of the upper classes; it might also have had the meaning ‘young man of a prominent family’, like Middle English child (see Child ).
Irish: in Ireland in many cases this is no doubt the English name, but in others it is possibly an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Bearáin ‘descendant of Bearán’, a byname meaning ‘spear’.

variants.name: Chill
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Derbyshire): variant of   Child   or   Chell  .

variants.name: Childs
meanings: English: variant of Child , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Chilton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Chilton, for example in Berkshire, Buckinghamshire, County Durham, Hampshire, Kent, Shropshire, Somerset, Suffolk, and Wiltshire. The majority are shown by early forms to derive from Old English cild ‘child’ (see Child ) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. One place of this name in Somerset possibly gets its first element from Old English cealc ‘chalk, limestone’, and one on the Isle of Wight from the personal name Cēola (compare Chilcott ), or from Old English ceole ‘deep valley’.

variants.name: Bambino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmine, Angelo.

Italian: from Bambino, a byname meaning ‘child’ (see Child ), or a personal name given with reference to Gesù Bambino ‘the Christ child’, the use of Gesù as a personal name being considered taboo in Italy.

variants.name: Chilson
meanings: English: habitational name from Chilson in Oxfordshire, named with Old English cild ‘young man’ (see Child ) + tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’.

History: It is not known when this surname was first brought to America, but it was already established in CT in the early 18th century. Daniel Chilson of Weathersfield, CT, was born c. 1720 and on 4 October 1745 married Sybil Stanclift in Middlesex County, CT.

variants.name: Fairchild
meanings: English (southern): nickname from Middle English fair child ‘handsome child’ or ‘handsome youth of noble birth’ (see Child ).

variants.name: Childers
meanings: English: probably a habitational name from some lost place called Childerhouse, from Old English cildra, genitive plural of cild ‘child’ + hūs ‘house’. This may have referred to some form of orphanage perhaps run by a religious order, or perhaps the first element is to be understood in its later sense as a term of status (see Child ).

nameMeanings.name: Johannah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Johannah Child was born in 1434, in Worcester, Worcestershire, England. She married William Childe about 1464, in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1511, in Northwick, Worcestershire, England, at the age of 77.

fr.lifeSummary: Johannah Child est née en 1434, à Worcester, Worcestershire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé William Childe environ 1464, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1511, à Northwick, Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 77 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Johannah Child wurde 1434 in Worcester, Worcestershire, England geboren. Sie hat um 1464 in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom William Childe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1511 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Northwick, Worcestershire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Johannah Child è nata il nel 1434 nel Worcester, Worcestershire, England. Lei sposato William Childe circa 1464, nel Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1511, nel Northwick, Worcestershire, England, all'età di 77 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Johannah Childは1434年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアウオルセスターで生まれた。約1464年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアでWilliam Childeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1511年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアノースウィックで77歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Johannah Child(은)는 1434년 영국 워스스터실르 워스스터에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1464년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르에서 William Childe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1511년 영국 워스스터실르 노르스윅에서 77세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Johannah Child родилась в 1434 г. в местности ворсестир, ворсестиршир, Англия. Она умерла в 1511 г. в местности нортвикк, ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 77 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Johannah Child nació en 1434, en Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Se casó con William Childe aproximadamente 1464, en Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
